structuredFacts.name: Kauno Kolegija (Kaunas University of Applied Sciences)
bestFitFor: Indian students who want an English-medium, practice-oriented nursing Bachelor's degree in the EU with heavy clinical internship exposure across multiple hospital levels
Students who prefer a large, well-established state higher education institution (nearly 5,000 students, 40+ programmes) over a small single-purpose college
Students who want to study in Kaunas, Lithuania's second-largest city, rather than the capital Vilnius, and value a large university-city student population

Students who plan to build a nursing career in Lithuania or elsewhere in the EU/EEA and are prepared to independently verify Indian Nursing Council (INC) equivalence before assuming the degree will be recognised for practice in India

programs.title: General Practice Nursing (Professional Bachelor of Health Sciences, General Nurse)
programs.notes: 3.5-year (7-semester, 210 ECTS) full-time programme delivered at the Faculty of Medicine in Kaunas. Curriculum (per Study in Lithuania's semester breakdown) covers Anatomy/Physiology/Pathology, Pharmacology, Microbiology and Infection Control, General/Therapeutic/Surgical/Geriatric/Maternity/Children's/Community/Mental Health Nursing, Emergency and Intensive Care, plus a sequence of Professional Internships in first-level practice centres, second-level hospitals, and third-level hospital departments, culminating in a Final Professional Internship and Final Thesis. Tuition fee at time of research: EUR 3,100/year (same for EU and non-EU students, per both the official application portal and Study in Lithuania); one-time application fee EUR 120 (non-refundable, invoiced only after document pre-approval). English proficiency required (IELTS >=5.5 / TOEFL >=75 / PTE >=59 / CEFR B2), with an institution-run English test (EUR 45) available if no certificate is held; a motivation interview and key-competence test are also part of admissions. Qualification awarded: Professional Bachelor of Health Sciences. Figures are as observed at time of research and should be reconfirmed for the intake year in question, since fees and requirements are reviewed periodically by Kauno kolegija.

structuredFacts.researchNotes: Non-WDOMS discovered university, added via overnight research workflow. Kauno Kolegija (Kaunas University of Applied Sciences, Lithuanian: Kauno kolegija) is a public, multi-profile Lithuanian university of applied sciences established in 2000 as part of Lithuania's binary higher-education reform, though its constituent schools trace roots to earlier institutions (including nursing-education courses dating to 1920 and 1941). It is one of the largest professional higher-education institutions in Lithuania, with roughly 4,900-5,500 students, 40+ study programmes (11 taught in English), campuses in Kaunas plus regional departments in Alytus and Tauragė, and 5 student dormitories. Its only clinically relevant programme for the India-outbound audience is the 3.5-year General Practice Nursing Professional Bachelor's, delivered at the Faculty of Medicine — there is no MBBS/medicine, pharmacy, or BDS programme at this institution, so only bsc-nursing is listed. Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognition of any foreign nursing qualification, including this one, is handled case-by-case via INC's equivalence process rather than being automatic — this page tells students to verify eligibility with INC before enrolling and does not assert automatic recognition. Tuition (EUR 3,100/year) and dormitory pricing (roughly EUR 80-180/month depending on room type and dormitory) are stated as observed at check time via two independent sources (the official DreamApply application portal and the national Study in Lithuania portal) and should be treated as indicative, subject to periodic review.

draftContent.summary: Kauno Kolegija, also known in English as Kaunas University of Applied Sciences, is a public Lithuanian university of applied sciences established in 2000 as part of Lithuania's binary higher-education reform, though several of its constituent schools have earlier roots, including nursing-education courses tracing back to the 1920s. It is one of the largest professional higher-education institutions in the country, with close to 5,000 students, over 40 study programmes (11 taught fully in English), and a main campus in Kaunas plus regional departments in Alytus and Tauragė. For students exploring healthcare study in the Baltics, its Faculty of Medicine runs a 3.5-year, English-medium General Practice Nursing programme leading to a Professional Bachelor of Health Sciences qualification, built around a dense sequence of clinical internships across primary-care centres, secondary hospitals, and tertiary hospital departments. Indian students considering this route should treat it as a nursing-specific option — Kauno Kolegija does not offer MBBS, pharmacy, or dental programmes — and should independently confirm current tuition, admission requirements, and Indian Nursing Council (INC) equivalence eligibility before committing, since foreign nursing qualifications are assessed by INC case-by-case rather than automatically recognised.
draftContent.campusLifestyle: Kauno Kolegija's Faculty of Medicine is one of several faculties spanning technology, informatics, engineering, humanities, social sciences, art, education, business, law, and agricultural sciences, giving Health Sciences students a genuinely multi-disciplinary campus community rather than a narrow single-subject college environment. The institution positions practical training as central to its identity, stating that hands-on coursework makes up at least one-third of total programme content across its degrees, and General Practice Nursing goes further with an extensive internship sequence spanning nearly every semester. Kauno Kolegija cooperates with more than 200 partner institutions across 38 countries and is a member of the UNINOVIS European University Alliance, giving nursing students realistic access to an Erasmus+ exchange semester or clinical placement abroad. With roughly 4,900 students, 17 academic buildings, and 70 workshops and laboratories, the campus operates at a scale that supports active student societies, sports facilities, and a functioning student self-governance structure, while still being organised around close staff-student contact in its practice-oriented teaching model.
draftContent.cityProfile: Kaunas is Lithuania's second-largest city, with a population of just over 300,000, sitting at the confluence of the country's two largest rivers, the Nemunas and the Neris. It served as Lithuania's temporary capital between 1920 and 1939 while Vilnius was under Polish control, a period that left the city with an exceptional concentration of interwar Art Deco and Lithuanian National Revival architecture — recognised in 2023 as a UNESCO World Heritage Site under the inscription 'Modernist Kaunas: Architecture of Optimism, 1919-1939.' Kaunas was also a joint European Capital of Culture in 2022. As home to several major Lithuanian universities beyond Kauno Kolegija, the city has a large, youthful resident student population, giving it an active café culture, student-oriented nightlife, and a compact, walkable historic centre. Compared to Vilnius, Kaunas is generally considered more affordable for day-to-day living while still offering a full range of shops, healthcare facilities, and public transport connections, including reasonably quick rail and bus links to the capital.
draftContent.clinicalExposure: The General Practice Nursing curriculum is structured around a progressive internship sequence that begins with a Professional Internship of General Nursing in the first year and continues through Therapeutic, Surgical, Geriatric, Emergency and Intensive Care, Mental Health, Maternity Care, Children's, and Community Nursing internships in later semesters, closing with a Final Professional Internship before the final thesis and examination. Per the national Study in Lithuania programme listing, these placements take place across first-level practice centres, second-level hospitals, and third-level (tertiary) hospital departments, giving students exposure to a genuine range of care complexity rather than a single facility type. Classroom subjects — Anatomy, Physiology, Pathology, Pharmacology, Microbiology and Infection Control, and applied nursing research — are sequenced to build toward each internship block. Because clinical placement sites can vary by cohort and by which partner healthcare institutions are active in a given academic year, students should confirm current placement hospitals directly with the Faculty of Medicine before applying, rather than assuming a fixed list of teaching hospitals.
draftContent.hostelOverview: Kauno Kolegija operates five student dormitories — four in Kaunas (Dormitories No. 1-4, at Taikos Ave. 121, V. Krėvės Ave. 90/92, and Kalniečių St. 126) and one in Alytus for students at that regional department. The Kaunas dormitories sit close to the Pramonės Ave. campus buildings and offer block-type rooms with private facilities, double rooms, and triple rooms (Dormitory No. 4 also has single rooms), with shared kitchens, showers, and toilets available on each floor. As listed on the official site at the time of research, monthly pricing ranges roughly from EUR 80-90 for triple rooms up to EUR 135-180 for single or block-type rooms, with additional one-time costs such as a refundable EUR 200 security deposit, a EUR 20 key/access-card fee, and optional paid extras (laundry, bedding rental, small appliance rental). International and Erasmus+ students reserve a place through the university's eDormitory online system once issued a university email account; students should confirm current room availability and pricing for their specific intake directly with Kauno Kolegija's accommodation coordinator before travel.
draftContent.indianFoodSupport: Kaunas does not have as large or established an Indian student and diaspora community as bigger Western European university cities, so students should not expect an extensive dedicated Indian grocery or restaurant scene comparable to major Indian-student hubs. That said, as Lithuania's second-largest city with a substantial multi-university student population, Kaunas has a reasonably international food scene, and larger supermarkets carry basic spices, rice, lentils, and international-aisle staples that support home cooking; dormitory kitchens allow students to cook for themselves. Students who rely heavily on Indian food should expect to supplement local shopping with spices brought from home or sourced from Vilnius (a short train or bus ride away) when travelling, and should connect with existing Indian or South Asian student groups at Kauno Kolegija or in Kaunas through the university's international office before arrival to get current, first-hand guidance on food access.
draftContent.safetyOverview: Kaunas is generally regarded as a safe, walkable city for students, consistent with Lithuania's broader reputation as a low-crime EU country, and its compact historic centre and strong public-transport network make day-to-day movement straightforward. As in any city, students should take standard precautions at night and around transit hubs, and should keep valuables secure in shared dormitory accommodation. Kauno Kolegija's non-academic student support includes accommodation services and access to psychological/wellbeing support as part of its stated student-support offering. Indian students should register their arrival and residence status as required under Lithuanian immigration rules, and should keep in touch with the university's international office and the Embassy of India in Vilnius for updated safety and consular guidance specific to their stay.
draftContent.studentSupport: Kauno Kolegija's official admissions channel (admission@go.kauko.lt) handles international application queries for programmes such as General Practice Nursing, while a separate accommodation coordinator manages dormitory placement and administration across the university's five dormitories. The university's own 'Student support' framework, as described on its official site, covers both academic support (integration and admission assistance, academic advising, library and career services, and support tailored to international learners) and non-academic support (financial assistance, disability/learning-difficulty support, accommodation services, leisure and sports programmes, and psychological support). As a UNINOVIS alliance member with 200+ partner institutions across 38 countries, the university also runs an active international-cooperation office supporting incoming and outgoing student mobility. Indian applicants should additionally plan to consult the Embassy of India in Vilnius for visa, residence-permit, and consular support, and should verify Indian Nursing Council (INC) equivalence requirements directly with INC before enrolling, since recognition of any foreign nursing qualification is assessed case-by-case rather than granted automatically.
whyChoose: Large, well-established public state university of applied sciences (established 2000, ~4,900-5,500 students, 40+ programmes) rather than a small single-purpose institution

General Practice Nursing is taught entirely in English, with a structured, near-continuous internship sequence spanning primary-care centres, secondary hospitals, and tertiary hospital departments across nearly every semester
UNINOVIS European University Alliance member with 200+ partner institutions in 38 countries, giving nursing students realistic access to Erasmus+ exchange or clinical placements abroad
Located in Kaunas, Lithuania's second-largest city and a UNESCO World Heritage Site (Modernist Kaunas), with a large resident student population and generally lower living costs than Vilnius
Comparatively affordable EU nursing degree at EUR 3,100/year tuition, with 5 university-run dormitories offering monthly rents from roughly EUR 80 upward
thingsToConsider: Kauno Kolegija offers only nursing among clinically relevant programmes for India-outbound students — there is no MBBS, pharmacy, or BDS pathway at this institution

Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognition of this (or any) foreign nursing degree is assessed case-by-case through INC's equivalence process, not automatically granted — students must verify eligibility with INC before enrolling
English proficiency must be demonstrated via IELTS/TOEFL/PTE/EnglishScore (or the university's own paid English test); a Medium of Instruction (MOI) letter alone is explicitly not accepted as proof
A motivation interview and key-competence test are mandatory parts of the admissions process, in addition to standard document submission
Kaunas has a smaller Indian student community than major European study hubs, so day-to-day Indian food access and peer community will be more limited than in bigger cities

Tuition (EUR 3,100/year), the EUR 120 application fee, and dormitory pricing (roughly EUR 80-180/month) were current at the time of research and should be re-confirmed for the specific intake year, since fees and requirements are periodically reviewed by the university

faq.question: Does Kauno Kolegija (Kaunas University of Applied Sciences) offer MBBS or a medical degree programme?
faq.answer: No. Kauno Kolegija is a public university of applied sciences with faculties spanning technology, informatics, health sciences, business, and other fields, and it does not run an MBBS or Doctor of Medicine programme. Its only clinically relevant health programme for India-outbound students is the 3.5-year General Practice Nursing Professional Bachelor's degree, delivered at its Faculty of Medicine. Students specifically seeking MBBS should look at Lithuania's dedicated medical universities instead.

faq.question: Is the General Practice Nursing programme at Kauno Kolegija taught in English?
faq.answer: Yes. Both the official application portal and the national Study in Lithuania programme listing confirm the language of instruction as English for this programme. Applicants must still demonstrate English proficiency (IELTS 5.5+, TOEFL 75+, PTE 59+, or CEFR B2) via a recognised test, or sit the university's own English test for a fee if they lack a certificate; a Medium of Instruction letter alone is not accepted.

faq.question: What does the General Practice Nursing programme at Kauno Kolegija cost, and how long does it take?
faq.answer: As listed on the official application portal and corroborated by Study in Lithuania at the time of research, the programme is 3.5 years (7 semesters, 210 ECTS) full-time, with tuition of EUR 3,100 per year (the same rate for EU and non-EU students) plus a one-time, non-refundable EUR 120 application fee. Students should confirm the current fee for their specific intake year directly with the university, since fees are subject to periodic review.

faq.question: Will a nursing degree from Kauno Kolegija be recognised by the Indian Nursing Council (INC)?
faq.answer: There is no automatic recognition. The Indian Nursing Council evaluates foreign nursing qualifications individually through its equivalence process, reviewing the syllabus and institutional details before granting registration eligibility in India. Students should start this verification with INC directly, ideally before enrolling, rather than assuming eligibility based on the degree alone.

faq.question: What kind of clinical training do General Practice Nursing students get at Kauno Kolegija?
faq.answer: The programme includes a dense sequence of Professional Internships spanning nearly every semester — covering general, therapeutic, surgical, geriatric, emergency/intensive care, mental health, maternity, children's, and community nursing — with placements in first-level practice centres, second-level hospitals, and third-level hospital departments, per the official curriculum breakdown. Because specific clinical partner sites can vary by cohort, students should confirm current placement institutions directly with the Faculty of Medicine before applying.

faq.question: What does student accommodation cost at Kauno Kolegija?
faq.answer: Kauno Kolegija operates five dormitories (four in Kaunas, one in its Alytus department), with room types ranging from shared triples to single rooms. As listed on the official site at the time of research, monthly rent ranges roughly from EUR 80-90 for triple rooms to EUR 135-180 for single or block-type rooms, plus a refundable EUR 200 security deposit and small optional service fees. International students reserve a room through the university's eDormitory system after receiving their university email account, and should confirm current pricing and availability for their intake year.

faq.question: What is Kaunas like as a city for international students?
faq.answer: Kaunas is Lithuania's second-largest city, located at the confluence of the Nemunas and Neris rivers, and served as the country's temporary capital from 1920 to 1939. Its extensive interwar Art Deco architecture was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2023, and the city was a joint European Capital of Culture in 2022. With several major universities based there, Kaunas has a large resident student population, a walkable historic centre, and generally lower living costs than the capital, Vilnius, though its Indian student community is smaller than in bigger European study hubs.
